oneLiner: The protein-packed, refreshingly light drink for fun, premium, social moments—no alcohol required.
positioningStatement: PEP is the light, premium protein drink that reinvents the social ritual without alcohol. Designed for group get-togethers—bars, dinners, rooftop parties, festivals—it delivers an exciting, shareable experience with vibrant, fruity taste and a fun, bold design. This is for people who want to feel good and have fun without sacrificing the occasion or their vibe.
audience: Social, urban 20–40 year olds seeking a sophisticated, alcohol-free drink out
People who prioritize experience, aesthetics, and inclusion in group settings
The 'fun leader' or social connector who brings interesting, non-boring drinks to parties, dinners, or festivals
occasions: At bars as a non-alc cocktail replacement
Pre-dinner aperitif or at lively group dinners
Dates and celebrations where alcohol feels heavy
Social gatherings where you want to join in—not be left with a plain soda

voice.tone: Vibrant, energetic, inclusive and irreverently premium. Always lively, never clinical. Celebrate togetherness and pleasure.

visual.mood: Vibrant, fun, upbeat, festival–rooftop party energy. Premium but not stuffy—inviting and extroverted.
visual.photography: Group shots, laughter, festival and nightlife scenes, up-close drinks with rich color and condensation. Zero solo fitness studio/bench/gym images.
visual.typography: Hero: Didot for logo (elegant, premium), Brittany Signature script for fun energy (tagline; ‘Feel good. Have fun.’), Montserrat for labels. Bebas Neue for callouts.
visual.designConcept: fun, premium packaging, vibrant, festival vibe, fruity
rejectLooks: Overly minimalist aesthetic with soft pastels (not social enough)
Children's drink vibes or cartoonish design

inLineBrands.name: Kin Euphorics
inLineBrands.why: Blends social appeal with mood-enhancing functionalities; product and branding are tailored for high-energy group occasions.

inLineBrands.name: Lyre's
inLineBrands.why: Alcohol-free spirit made for cocktail moments, with elegant, social, and fun spirit bottle cues.

inLineBrands.name: Seedlip
inLineBrands.why: Premium, non-alcoholic, facilitates the ritual and aesthetic of social drinking with high-sheen design.

inLineBrands.name: Ole cocktails
inLineBrands.why: Premium festive RTD cocktails that fit the vibrant, group-occasion energy; modern visual grammar matches PEP’s sociable lane.

positioningFitTypeMismatch.name: Protochol
positioningFitTypeMismatch.whyBrandingFits: Straddles social and functional; introduces protein to the bar world with a fun, light blue package and party-first messaging.
positioningFitTypeMismatch.typeMismatch: Alcohol-containing (not non-alc); not a pure protein RTD for social settings.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: HOLY
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Functional vitamin drink targeting health and hydration routines over social occasions; lacks social, experiential focus.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: LMNT
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Electrolyte mix heavily positioned around sports hydration and fitness. Does not address social experience, mood, or occasion.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: NOCCO
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Sports drink for workouts/training—performance over pleasure. No social, aesthetic, or group ritual context.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: VITAMIN WELL
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Hydration with vitamins, focused on health optimization, not on social drinking or enjoyable ritual.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: BLOOM
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Lifestyle, gut health, and wellness cues; lacks the group-focused, vibrant social experience required here.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: EVER TREE
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Minimalist, premium soft drink for everyday wellness moments; not vibrant or ritualistic enough for true social 'event' energy.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: OLIPOP
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Wellness/prebiotic soda — brand works for healthy lifestyles, not for out-of-home group events or party experience.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Poppi
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Prebiotic wellness-oriented; playful and lively, but ultimately not a 'ritual drink' for bars or parties.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Alive & Well
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Kombucha with friendly, approachable wellness tone. Great for healthy casual moments, not optimized for the ritual/party social occasion.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Recess
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Lifestyle/wellness-driven, relaxation and stress relief; pastel and soft, lacking the vibrancy and high-energy required for PEP.

peerBrandsOtherPositionings.name: Ish
peerBrandsOtherPositionings.whyNotActive: Non-alc mocktail brand with great authenticity; design is minimalist-contemporary, but overall more at-home/wellness, not full-on event/ritual.

antiReferences.name: Lean Body / gym RTD
antiReferences.why: Too ‘fit’, bro-y, and functional. Gym and muscle cues signal workout first, not social fun.

antiReferences.name: drinkpep.com (US)
antiReferences.why: Name collision—completely different product (energy/probiotic/US). Absolutely separate brand/digital presence.

brandFitRules: If the primary context is fitness, recovery, or health routine, reject as an in-line peer for PEP.
If visual cues are muted, clinical, or overly minimalist (pastels, sanitized), reject for social line.
Only accept peers that are specifically marketed for social occasions (bars, festivals, parties, group rituals), even if non-alc.
If packaging looks like traditional energy/protein shake or displays gym/sport cues, classification is anti-reference.
Prebiotics, kombucha, wellness soft drinks are not in-line unless they match group occasion, vibrant, ritualistic energy.
Never copy names/logos/claims from anti-references or unrelated product domains.
